source_code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

class MAMLStep(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, model):
        super().__init__()
        self.model = model
        self.loss_fn = nn.MSELoss()

    def step(self, x, y, x_q, y_q):
        # BUG: first-order computation: inner loss backward frees the graph,
        # then trying to take second-order grads on outer loss fails.
        preds = self.model(x)
        loss = self.loss_fn(preds, y)
        loss.backward()  # frees intermediates
        # now compute query loss with create_graph=True on the SAME graph
        preds_q = self.model(x_q)
        loss_q = self.loss_fn(preds_q, y_q)
        grads = torch.autograd.grad(loss_q, self.model.parameters(), create_graph=True)
        return grads

def run():
    m = nn.Linear(8, 1)
    step = MAMLStep(m)
    x = torch.randn(16, 8)
    y = torch.randn(16, 1)
    return step.step(x, y, x, y)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()
expected_fix_hint: Pass retain_graph=True on the inner .backward(), or replace .backward() with torch.autograd.grad(..., create_graph=True) that preserves the graph.
